headline: 18 on-chain addresses catalogued across Ethereum, Arbitrum, Polygon, Avalanche, and BNB Chain via Etherscan/block-explorer name-tags and OKX DEX docs; no canonical deployed-contracts page found; OKX is a CEX with custodial hot/cold wallets and an on-chain DEX aggregator component.

findings.text: Etherscan name-tag search yielded: OKX main EOA (0x6cc5f688), OKX 24 (0xbf94f0ac), OKX 20 (0x7eb6c83a), OKX Hot Wallet (0x4b4e14a3), OKX Hot Wallet 2 (0x4e7b1103), OKX Hot Wallet 3 (0xa9ac43f5), OKX Cold Wallet (0x611f7bf8), OKX Deposit Funder (0xc5a93444), OKX wallet (0x96fdc631), OKX 3 EOA (0xa7efae72), OKB Token proxy (0x75231f58), DEX Token Approval 1 (0x40aa958d), DEX Aggregation Router (0x3b3ae790), DEX Router (0x2e1dee21), DEX Router 2 (0x7d0ccaa3), OKX NFT 3 (0xa7fd9974). Arbiscan yielded DEX Router (0x01d8edb8) and DEX Token Approval 6 (0x70cbb871) on Arbitrum. PolygonScan yielded OKX 1 (0x06959153).

No single canonical 'Deployed Contracts' page found at okx.com or web3.okx.com. OKX's Proof of Reserves page (https://www.okx.com/proof-of-reserves/download) provides downloadable CSVs with wallet addresses, but does not enumerate smart contract addresses by role. The DEX smart contract docs page (https://web3.okx.com/onchainos/dev-docs/trade/dex-smart-contract) references router and approval addresses per chain but does not render a full static table. deployed_contracts_doc set to the PoR download page as the closest approximation.

No audit PDF links recorded in protocol.audit_links. Hacken conducts monthly Proof of Reserves audits for OKX (confirmed via hacken.io/case-studies/okx-por/). No smart-contract audit PDFs were surfaced in this run.

OKX GitHub org at https://github.com/okx confirmed, including proof-of-reserves repo. OKX Labs org references DEX-Router-EVM-V1 (https://github.comxlabs/DEX-Router-EVM-V1) in official docs. No hardhat-deploy or Foundry broadcast artifacts surfaced at pinned SHAs in this run; GitHub SHAs not pinned.

Multi-chain enumeration: Ethereum — 14 addresses surfaced (hot/cold wallets, DEX contracts, OKB token). Arbitrum — DEX Router (0x01d8edb8) and DEX Token Approval (0x70cbb871) confirmed via arbiscan.io. Polygon — OKX 1 (0x06959153) and DEX Token Approval (0x3B86917369) via OKX DEX docs API response. Avalanche — DEX Token Approval (0x40aA958dd8) shared with Ethereum per OKX docs. Bitcoin, Solana, Tron, Doge, Ripple, Litecoin, TON, Aptos, Polkadot — non-EVM chains; no smart contract addresses surfaced (expected for CEX hot/cold wallets on non-EVM chains). Base, Linea, zkSync Era, Scroll, Starknet, Optimism — no specific named-tag addresses surfaced beyond those shared with other chains in OKX DEX docs; zero confirmed this run.

DEX Aggregation Router (0x3b3ae790 on Ethereum) is a factory-pattern aggregator routing through external DEX pools; no child-pool enumeration method applicable. OKX NFT 3 contract (0xa7fd9974) was created by factory 0x415cf13930bbb73c0cee7f8566144d0ef8b53518 per Etherscan; NFT contract factory enumeration not attempted (out of scope for CEX discovery).

Role taxonomy applied: custodial EOA wallets classified as 'other' (hot/cold wallet); OKB token proxy classified as 'token'; DEX router/approval contracts classified as 'router'; DEX aggregation router classified as 'router'. OKB proxy has an upgradeability owner (unknown EOA); actor_class set to 'unknown' pending on-chain read.
All addresses in admin_addresses[] trace to fetched Etherscan, Arbiscan, Polygonscan, or OKX DEX docs URLs. No snippet-only sightings promoted to admin_addresses.

rationale.verdict: Discovery run catalogued 18 unique addresses across Ethereum (14), Arbitrum (2), Polygon (2, shared with Ethereum for token approval), sourced from Etherscan/Arbiscan/Polygonscan name-tags and the OKX DEX supported-chains API response. No canonical deployed-contracts doc exists; non-EVM chains (Bitcoin, Solana, Tron, etc.) yield no discoverable smart contract addresses as expected for a CEX. OKB token is an upgradeable proxy. grade=unknown is correct for a discovery slice.

OKX official Proof of Reserves page with downloadable CSV files containing wallet addresses across Ethereum, Optimism, Arbitrum, Polygon, Avalanche and other chains
OKX bug bounty program on HackenProof; rewards up to $1M USDT for extreme vulnerabilities; covers OKX Web3 Wallet and smart contracts
OKX Labs onchain bug bounty on Cantina/Spearbit covering production smart contracts deployed by OKX Labs
Hacken conducts monthly dynamic Proof of Reserves audits for OKX; covers assets across 20+ networks including Ethereum, Solana, Polygon, Bitcoin; zk-STARK verification used
OKX describes semi-offline multi-signature hot wallet mechanism and air-gapped cold wallet system; cold wallet private keys stored offline in encrypted form
OKX DEX smart contract docs noting router and token approval addresses are subject to replacement due to upgrades; references github.com/okxlabs/DEX-Router-EVM-V1 for ABI
OKX formerly known as OKEx; cryptocurrency exchange headquartered in San Jose CA; founded by Star Xu in 2013; rebranded to OKX in January 2022

unknowns: D2: No canonical static 'Deployed Contracts' page found at okx.com or docs; PoR CSV download is the closest approximation but is not a structured address registry
D3: No audit PDFs recorded in protocol.audit_links; smart-contract audit reports for OKX DEX router contracts not surfaced in this run; only Hacken PoR attestation found
D4: GitHub repos at github.com/okx and github.com/okxlabs confirmed but no pinned commit SHA obtained for deployment artifacts; run-latest.json or hardhat-deploy artifacts not surfaced

D5: Base-offchain: OKX-tagged addresses on Base, Linea, zkSync Era, Scroll, Starknet, Optimism not surfaced via block-explorer name-tag search in this run; on-chain fetch of basescan.org/lineascan.build/era.zksync.network for OKX name-tag not attempted due to search quota exhaustion

D5: Bitcoin, Solana, Tron, Doge, Ripple, Litecoin, TON, Aptos, Polkadot: zero smart-contract addresses expected (non-EVM chains used for CEX custody only); raw wallet addresses appear in PoR CSV but were not extracted this run
D6: OKX DEX Aggregation Router (0x3b3ae790 on Ethereum) is an upgradeable proxy — proxy admin address not read this run; control/upgrade authority unknown
D6: OKB Token proxy upgrade owner (proxyOwnerPosition slot of 0x75231f58) not read this run; actor class of proxy admin unknown
D7: OKX 20 (0x7eb6c83a), OKX 3 (0xa7efae72), OKX 24 (0xbf94f0ac) labelled as generic EOAs; their specific roles (deposit sweeper, fee collector, etc.) not determined
D8: OKX NFT factory address 0x415cf13930bbb73c0cee7f8566144d0ef8b53518 surfaced from Etherscan but not fully verified as OKX-controlled vs third-party; kept in unknowns

admin_addresses.role: router (DEX Aggregation Router on BNB Chain — TransparentUpgradeableProxy)
other (DEX token approval on BNB Chain)

protocol_metadata.about: OKX is a centralized cryptocurrency exchange (CEX) offering spot, margin, perpetual, options, and futures trading across hundreds of tokens, where user assets are held in custody via OKX-controlled hot and cold wallet systems employing semi-offline multi-signature security. Alongside the custodial exchange, OKX operates an on-chain DEX aggregator (OKX DEX) deployed as upgradeable smart contracts on Ethereum and 15+ EVM-compatible chains, routing trades through external liquidity sources. OKX publishes monthly Proof of Reserves reports using zk-STARK cryptographic attestation, audited by Hacken, to allow users to verify that reserves exceed liabilities. The OKB token (Ethereum proxy contract 0x75231f58) is OKX's native exchange token used for fee discounts and ecosystem participation.
